children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established a Human Smuggling and Trafficking Center (referred to in this section as the “Center”).

children.heading: Operation
children.label: b Operation
children.text: The Secretary of State, the Secretary of Homeland Security, and the Attorney General shall operate the Center in accordance with the Memorandum of Understanding entitled, “Human Smuggling and Trafficking Center (HSTC), Charter”.
serve as the focal point for interagency efforts to integrate and disseminate intelligence and information related to terrorist travel;

serve as a clearinghouse with respect to all relevant information from all Federal Government agencies in support of the United States strategy to prevent separate, but related, issues of clandestine terrorist travel and facilitation of migrant smuggling and trafficking of persons;

ensure cooperation among all relevant policy, law enforcement, diplomatic, and intelligence agencies of the Federal Government to improve effectiveness and to convert all information available to the Federal Government relating to clandestine terrorist travel and facilitation, migrant smuggling, and trafficking of persons into tactical, operational, and strategic intelligence that can be used to combat such illegal activities; and

prepare and submit to Congress, on an annual basis, a strategic assessment regarding vulnerabilities in the United States and foreign travel system that may be exploited by international terrorists, human smugglers and traffickers, and their facilitators.

children.heading: Director
children.label: d Director
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall nominate an official of the Government of the United States to serve as the Director of the Center, in accordance with the requirements of the memorandum of understanding entitled the “Human Smuggling and Trafficking Center (HSTC) Charter”.

children.label: i
children.text: The Office of Intelligence and Analysis.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Transportation Security Administration.

children.label: iii
children.text: United States Citizenship and Immigration Services.

children.label: iv
children.text: United States Customs and Border Protection.

children.label: v
children.text: The United States Coast Guard.

children.label: vi
children.text: United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement.

children.label: i
children.text: The Central Intelligence Agency.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Department of Defense.

children.label: iii
children.text: The Department of the Treasury.

children.label: iv
children.text: The National Counterterrorism Center.

children.label: v
children.text: The National Security Agency.

children.label: vi
children.text: The Department of Justice.

children.label: vii
children.text: The Department of State.

children.label: viii
children.text: Any other relevant agency or department.

children.label: A
children.text: intelligence analysts or special agents with demonstrated experience related to human smuggling, trafficking in persons, or terrorist travel; and

children.label: i
children.text: consular affairs;

children.label: ii
children.text: counterterrorism;

children.label: iii
children.text: criminal law enforcement;

children.label: iv
children.text: intelligence analysis;

children.label: v
children.text: prevention and detection of document fraud;

children.label: vi
children.text: border inspection;

children.label: vii
children.text: immigration enforcement; or

children.label: viii
children.text: human trafficking and combating severe forms of trafficking in persons.

children.heading: Incentives for service in certain positions
children.label: A Incentives for service in certain positions

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security, and the heads of other relevant agencies, shall prescribe regulations or promulgate personnel policies to provide incentives for service on the staff of the Center, particularly for serving terms of at least two years duration.

children.heading: Forms of incentives
children.label: ii Forms of incentives
children.text: Incentives under clause (i) may include financial incentives, bonuses, and such other awards and incentives as the Secretary and the heads of other relevant agencies, consider appropriate.1So in original. The comma probably should not appear.1

children.heading: Enhanced promotion for service at the Center
children.label: B Enhanced promotion for service at the Center
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Homeland Security, and the heads of other relevant agencies, shall ensure that personnel who are assigned or detailed to service at the Center shall be considered for promotion at rates equivalent to or better than similarly situated personnel of such agencies who are not so assigned or detailed, except that this subparagraph shall not apply in the case of personnel who are subject to the provisions of the Foreign Service Act of 1980 [ et seq.].22 U.S.C. 3901

children.heading: Administrative support and funding
children.label: f Administrative support and funding
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall provide to the Center the administrative support and funding required for its maintenance, including funding for personnel, leasing of office space, supplies, equipment, technology, training, and travel expenses necessary for the Center to carry out its functions.

children.heading: Initial report
children.label: 1 Initial report
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the President shall transmit to Congress a report regarding the implementation of this section, including a description of the staffing and resource needs of the Center.December 17, 2004

children.label: A
children.text: the roles and responsibilities of each agency or department that is participating in the Center;

children.label: B
children.text: the mechanisms used to share information among each such agency or department;

children.label: C
children.text: the personnel provided to the Center by each such agency or department;

children.label: D
children.text: the type of information and reports being disseminated by the Center;

children.label: E
children.text: any efforts by the Center to create a centralized Federal Government database to store information related to unlawful travel of foreign nationals, including a description of any such database and of the manner in which information utilized in such a database would be collected, stored, and shared;

children.label: F
children.text: how each agency and department shall utilize its resources to ensure that the Center uses intelligence to focus and drive its efforts;

children.label: G
children.text: efforts to consolidate networked systems for the Center;

children.label: H
children.text: the mechanisms for the sharing of homeland security information from the Center to the Office of Intelligence and Analysis, including how such sharing shall be consistent with ;section 485(b) of title 6

children.label: I
children.text: the ability of participating personnel in the Center to freely access necessary databases and share information regarding issues related to human smuggling, trafficking in persons, and terrorist travel;

children.label: J
children.text: how the assignment of personnel to the Center is incorporated into the civil service career path of such personnel; and

children.label: K
children.text: cooperation and coordination efforts, including any memorandums of understanding, among participating agencies and departments regarding issues related to human smuggling, trafficking in persons, and terrorist travel.

children.heading: Relationship to the NCTC
children.label: h Relationship to the NCTC
children.text: As part of its mission to combat terrorist travel, the Center shall work to support the efforts of the National Counterterrorism Center.

children.heading: Coordination with the Office of Intelligence and Analysis
children.label: i Coordination with the Office of Intelligence and Analysis
children.text: The Office of Intelligence and Analysis, in coordination with the Center, shall submit to relevant State, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies periodic reports regarding terrorist threats related to human smuggling, human trafficking, and terrorist travel.
